hazard.summary: Anthropic long-context billing gate returns HTTP 429 with no Retry-After header and no time-bound signal. Message body is the only classification signal. Correct hazard class (quota_exhausted) was identified but enforcement action was scoped to the API credential instead of the billing gate, blocking all provider requests for 30 minutes including models unaffected by the long-context gate.
signals: HTTP 429 with body: "Extra usage is required for long context requests."
No Retry-After header present — confirmed by header extraction trace
No x-ratelimit-reset-requests or x-ratelimit-reset-tokens headers present
Message body is the only distinguishing signal available
Provider-wide credential backoff applied for 30 minutes
Subsequent requests to unaffected models blocked internally before reaching Anthropic

constraints: Message body must be inspected when Retry-After is absent — status code alone is insufficient.
Billing gate errors must not trigger credential-scoped backoff — the gate is account-level, not credential-level.
Backoff scope must match the scope of the error condition: long-context gate → model/feature scope, not provider-wide credential scope.
A quota_exhausted classification must not automatically inherit credential backoff behavior.
knobs: backoff scope: credential vs model vs feature gate
verification: Verify subsequent requests to claude-opus-4-6 succeed immediately.
Verify no provider-wide credential backoff is applied.
Verify Retry-After absence is handled without fallback to credential backoff.
Verify message-body classification correctly identifies billing gate vs transient rate limit.
notes: This receipt documents two distinct failure shapes in one case: (1) message-only classification — the billing gate sends no Retry-After, making the error body the sole classification signal; (2) correct class, wrong enforcement scope — quota_exhausted was correctly identified but BACKOFF_QUOTA_EXHAUSTED_MS was applied to the API credential rather than the specific billing gate. The prior fix (PR #3257) corrected the error type from rate_limit to quota_exhausted but made the observable impact worse by extending backoff from 30 seconds to 30 minutes. Confirmed by author tracing header extraction path in anthropic-shared.js — retryAfterMs absent from persisted error entry.

constraints: message body is the classification signal when Retry-After is absent
billing gate backoff must not scope to credential
correct hazard class does not guarantee correct enforcement scope
anti_patterns: applying credential backoff to account-level billing gates
inheriting backoff duration from hazard class without checking scope
assuming Retry-After presence for all quota_exhausted cases
